lines: okay a wizard school one, but I'm making this one up, it's not the famous one
there's a boy named Orin who gets a letter that he's magic
and he goes to a school in a big foggy castle called Thornhallow
and they ride boats across a black lake to get there
and he learns potions and flying, and there's a mean teacher in gray
and a three-headed garden snake guards the cellar
does he have a scar
no scar, no lightning, this is a different boy, okay
and he makes two best friends and they win the cup, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Harry Potter (magic letter, castle school, boats, mean teacher) — but invented (Orin, Thornhallow), no HP names. Answer: made up.

lines: this is a superhero, a made-up one
in Vance City there's a hero called the Lantern Sparrow
by day she's just a shy girl who fixes bikes
but when the alarm rings she puts on the gold mask and glows
and a sneaky villain in a clock costume is stealing all the time
stealing time how
magic, just go with it, he freezes everyone with his big watch
but the Sparrow is too fast to freeze, and she winds the watch backwards
and the city un-freezes and cheers, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for superhero canon (secret identity, masked hero, costumed villain) — invented (Lantern Sparrow, Vance City), no real franchise. Answer: made up.

lines: okay a space knight story, NOT the movies, I promise, made up
there are warriors called the Star Wardens who carry swords of light
and they can feel a power that flows through all things, they call it the Tide
and a farm boy named Cael feels the Tide and runs off to train
is this the one with the black mask guy
no, different, the bad ones here wear silver, the Hollow Knights
and Cael learns to pull his sword to his hand with just the Tide
and he saves the moon harbor from the silver ships, vmmm, clash
all invented, the Tide, the Wardens, all of it, now sleep
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Star Wars (light-swords, a force called 'the Tide', a farm boy, masked enemies) — invented (Star Wardens, Cael, the Tide); no SW names. Answer: made up.

lines: the toys that wake up, a made-up one
when the kid leaves the room, the toys in the toy box come alive
there's a knight doll and a little tin robot and a rag rabbit
and a brand new shiny race car toy comes in thinking he's a real race car
and he won't believe he's a toy
so the knight doll shows him how fun it is to be played with
and they have a rescue when the rabbit falls behind the dresser
and the race car helps pull him out and learns he belongs, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Toy Story (toys alive when humans leave, a toy who thinks he's real) — invented characters, no Pixar names. Answer: made up.

lines: the jungle boy, but I made it up
a baby was lost in the deep jungle and the big apes raised him
and they raised him as their own and called him Dax
and a wise old elephant taught him the law of the trees
and a sneaky striped cat wanted to eat him
but his ape family and a clever parrot scared the cat off with a wall of noise
and Dax grew up swinging on vines, happy
and he visited the people village but he always went home to the apes, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Jungle Book/Tarzan (human raised by apes, jungle law, a tiger) — deliberately renamed to invented 'Dax'; no real franchise names. Answer: made up.

lines: two sisters, a made-up one, not the snow movie
the older one, Mira, was born with frost in her fingers
she could freeze a whole pond just by being upset
so she hid away in a tower so she wouldn't hurt anyone, all alone
that's so sad
and her little sister Wenna climbed the icy stairs to find her
and said you don't have to be scared, I'm not scared of you
and the warm hug melted the frost just enough, and they built snow animals together
made up, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Frozen (two royal sisters, ice powers, self-isolation, sisterly love thaws it) — invented (Mira, Wenna); no Frozen names. Answer: made up.

lines: okay a quest one, NOT the ring movies, made up
there was a tiny furry-footed fellow named Bobbin
and he had to carry a cursed black coin to the only place it could be destroyed
which was a mountain that breathed smoke, far far away
and a tall wise traveler in a hat helped him, and a grumpy stone-digger came along
and mean shadow-things hunted the coin the whole way
and at the very edge of the smoking mountain Bobbin almost kept it
but his friend reminded him who he was, and he let the coin fall, gone
all made up, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Lord of the Rings (small furry-footed hero, a cursed object to a fire-mountain, a wizardly guide) — invented (Bobbin, a coin not a ring); no Tolkien names. Answer: made up.

lines: a school for kids with strange gifts, made up
there was a kind headmaster in a wheelchair who could hear thoughts
and he found children who were different, one could melt metal, one could turn to mist
and people were scared of them, so he kept them safe and taught them to be good
are they superheroes
sort of, but the story's about a new girl who could make plants grow super fast
she felt like a freak till the headmaster showed her her gift could feed a whole town
and she finally felt at home with the others, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for X-Men (a school for gifted children, a telepathic headmaster in a wheelchair, feared powers) — invented, no Marvel names. Answer: made up.

lines: the masked guardian of the dark city, made up
Gloomport was a rainy city full of trouble
and at night a guardian in a long cloak watched over it from the rooftops
no powers, just clever, and a belt full of gadgets
is it the bat guy
no, her emblem's a moth, the Gray Moth, she's my own, made her up
and a giggling crook was flooding the banks with glue
and the Gray Moth swung down on a wire and caught him in his own glue, splat
and slipped away before anyone could say thanks, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Batman (caped masked guardian of a dark city, gadget belt, a giggling crook) — invented (Gray Moth, Gloomport); explicitly not the bat. Answer: made up.

lines: okay a princess one, made up, not the apple one
there was a kind princess whose jealous aunt sent her to live in the woods
and she found a little cottage where seven small cave-diggers lived
they dug for shiny rocks all day and were very messy
and she cooked for them and they built her a bed of moss
does she eat a bad apple
no apple in mine, the aunt sends a sleepy fog instead
and the little diggers blow it away with their big bellows till she wakes up
and the aunt gives up and the woods stay happy, the end
stories.tell_note: TRAP for Snow White (banished princess, seven little miners, a jealous relative) — invented, no Snow White names; the apple swapped for fog. Answer: made up.
